hypothesis: First measurement of gallery-130 identification on SCface under the current ArcFace / REMATCH=0.20 config (unchanged from run_016). Phase 2 of the hardening: tests the audit's prediction that gallery-1 success_rate overstates live-pipeline performance. Reports rank-1 gk accept (production number), rank-1 emb (embedder ceiling ignoring REMATCH), and rank-5/10 emb (lookalike crowding). Expected: d3_close rank-1 remains near-ceiling; d2_mid near-ceiling; d1_far drops materially from run_016's gallery-1 91 % as 129 distractors compete for the top slot.

notes: Baseline for gallery-N identification. BIG FINDING: at REMATCH=0.20, enrolling 130 distinct SCface subjects produces only 84 unique identities (46 subjects / 35 % merge into existing identities because cross-subject frontal-mugshot cosines sit above 0.20). The verification harness (gallery=1) could not see this by construction. On the surviving 84 enrolled subjects: rank1_gk=0.938 / 1.000 / 0.988 (d1/d2/d3), rank1_emb=0.963 / 1.000 / 1.000, rank5_emb=0.988 / 1.000 / 1.000. REMATCH is NOT the bottleneck on the surviving-enrolled path (gap between rank1_gk and rank1_emb is only 2-3 pts); the enrollment dedup collision IS. In production, any of the 46 merged walkers returning would be confidently matched to the wrong pre-existing identity. S1 floor 100 %. Decision=inconclusive (baseline — no knob diff), but this run hard-invalidates run_016’s keep: the 91 % gallery-1 d1_far number masks a 35 % identity-merge rate at the same REMATCH in a real multi-identity deployment. run_018 should revert REMATCH to 0.30 and re-measure dedup collisions to confirm the root cause.
